LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS JANE GAMP, PAT ALLEY AND SAN FRANCISCO LAW SCHOOL'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. DEFENDANTS' DEMURRER TO THE SECOND, THIRD, AND SIXTH CAUSES OF ACTION ARE SUSTAINED FOR FAILURE TO ALLEGE FACTS SUFFICIENT TO STATE A CAUSE OF ACTION (CCP 430.10(E)). AS TO THE SIXTH CAUSE OF ACTION, THE DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED AS TO DEFENDANTS JANE O'HARA GAMP AND PAT ALLEY ONLY WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. PLAINTIFF HAS NOT PLEAD FACTS SUFFICIENT TO ESTABLISH THAT GAMP AND ALLEY, AS EMPLOYEES OF SAN FRANCISCO LAW SCHOOL, WERE PARTIES TO THE CONTRACT, AND THEREFORE, THEY ARE NOT SUBJECT TO THE IMPLIED COVENANT OF GOOD FAITH AND FAIR DEALING. (SEE CLEARY V. AMERICAN AIRLINES, INC. (1980) 111 CAL.APP.3D 443, 456.) AS TO THE SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION FOR VIOLATION OF BUSINESS & PROFESSIONS CODE 17200, THE DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND. PLAINTIFF HAS NOT PLEAD FACTS SUFFICIENT TO ESTABLISH THAT MEMBERS OF THE PUBLIC ARE LIKELY TO BE DECEIVED. AS TO THE THIRD CAUSE OF ACTION, THE DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND. PLAINTIFF HAS NOT PLEAD FACTS SUFFICIENT TO ALLEGE A VIOLATION OF A FEDERAL OR STATE CONSTITUTIONAL RIGHT BY DEFENDANTS. THE DEMURRER TO THE FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION FOR BREACH OF CONTRACT IS OVERRULED; PLAINTIFF HAS ALLEGED FACTS SUFFICIENT TO STATE A CLAIM. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY C. VAUGHN, CSR # 6185

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jan-09-2014 Continued To Mar-05-14 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Dec-23-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
DEFENDANTS JANE GAMP'S, PAT ALLEY'S, and SAN FRANCISCO LAW SCHOOL'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is Overruled. As to the second cause of action, the First Amended Complaint ("FAC") adequately pleads that Defendants engaged in deceptive practices. In order to adequately plead a violation of Bus. & Prof. Code 17200, the complaint must state facts that establish the practice is unlawful, unfair, or deceptive. (Byars v. SCME Mortgage Bankers, Inc. (2003) 109 Cal.App.4th 1134, 1147)(emphasis added.).) In order to show that a practice was deceptive, the complaint must show on its face that members of the public are likely to be deceived. (South Bay Chevrolet v. General Motors Acceptance Corp. (1999) 72 Cal.App.4th 861, 878.) The FAC pleads that members of the public were likely to be deceived by misleading and false statements published by Defendants. (FAC, 37.) The FAC adequately pleads that Plaintiff has standing to maintain these claims, as the FAC alleges that Plaintiff was injured by Defendants' misleading statements. (FAC, 37b, "After using the false and misleading statements to get Plaintiff to enroll at SFLS'") (See Branick v. Downey Savings and Loan Assoc. (2006) 39 Cal.4th 235, 240.) As to the Third Cause of action, the FAC sufficiently pleads facts to establish a violation of Plaintiff's Equal Protection rights. The FAC pleads state action. (FAC, 42c.) The FAC pleads that Plaintiff was discriminated against because of her "race, color, gender, age, and national and ethnic origin." (FAC, 42e.) As to the sixth cause of action, the Court has already ruled that this cause of action is sustained without leave to amend as to Defendants Jane O'Hara Gamp and Pat Alley. (Order re: Demurrer to Complaint dated October 1, 2013.) While Plaintiff could have removed allegations pertaining to this cause of action from the First Amended Complaint, it is not required, as the record in this case reflects that this is not a viable cause of action. Plaintiff's request to amend the FAC to correct an error is denied. Plaintiff must seek leave of court for such amendment through a regularly noticed motion. (CCP 473(a)(1).) ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185 =302/MJM

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Oct-01-2013 Continued To Dec-18-13 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Sep-17-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance on this matter.

RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling, which was not contested, on DEFENDANTS JANE GAMP, PAT ALLEY and SAN FRANCISCO LAW SCHOOL'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Defendants' demurrer to the second, third, and sixth causes of action are sustained for failure to allege facts sufficient to state a cause of action (CCP 430.10(e)). As to the sixth cause of action, the demurrer is sustained as to Defendants Jane O'Hara Gamp and Pat Alley only without leave to amend. Plaintiff has not plead facts sufficient to establish that Gamp and Alley, as employees of San Francisco Law School, were parties to the contract, and therefore, they are not subject to the implied covenant of good faith and fair dealing. (See Cleary v. American Airlines, Inc. (1980) 111 Cal.App.3d 443, 456.) As to the second cause of action for violation of Business & Professions Code 17200, the demurrer is sustained with leave to amend. Plaintiff has not plead facts sufficient to establish that members of the public are likely to be deceived. As to the third cause of action, the demurrer is sustained with leave to amend. Plaintiff has not plead facts sufficient to allege a violation of a Federal or state constitutional right by defendants. The demurrer to the first cause of action for breach of contract is overruled; Plaintiff has alleged facts sufficient to state a claim. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185
